channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Australian government to gradually reduce EV tax incentives over three years
item.description: Sales of electric vehicles (EVs) in Australia have reached record levels, accounting for 27.5 per cent of light vehicle sales in April 2026, up from 23 per cent in March and 14 per cent a year ago. Th...

item.title: Government Warns Climate Change Could Hinder Tanzania's Blue Economy and Renewable Energy Goals
item.description: Tanzania Government is raising alarm say climate change fit slow down growth for country's blue economy, tourism and renewable energy sectors if nothing serious done. Permanent Secretary Richard Muyun...

item.title: Stars Shine on the Met Gala Red Carpet Celebrating ‘Fashion is Art’
item.description: The 2026 Met Gala, held at the Metropolitan Museum of Art in New York City on May 4, was a dazzling celebration of fashion as art. The event, famously dubbed the biggest night in fashion, drew an arra...

item.title: Raiders adjust squad ahead of Panthers rematch after key injuries and returns
item.description: The Canberra Raiders have made notable adjustments to their squad ahead of the highly anticipated rematch against the Penrith Panthers at Canberra Stadium this Sunday. Following last season's memorabl...

item.title: Investigation Launched into Workplace Culture at NSW Greyhound Racing Regulator
item.description: The NSW Premier’s Department has initiated an inquiry into the management and workplace culture of the Greyhound Welfare and Integrity Commission (GWIC) following allegations of a toxic work environme...

item.title: BFAR Provides Batanes Fisherfolk with Modern Vessel and Fuel Support
item.description: The Bureau of Fisheries and Aquatic Resources (BFAR) recently provided significant support to fishing communities in Batanes to help them cope with the challenges of high oil prices and rough sea cond...

item.title: Kano NDC Chairman Reject Handover of Party Structure to Rabiu Kwankwaso
item.description: Na serious crisis wey dey brew inside Kano State chapter of di Nigeria Democratic Congress (NDC) as di state chairman, Usaini Mai-Riga, come out openly to reject any plan to hand over di party leaders...

item.title: Highlights from the 2026 Met Gala: When Fashion Meets Art
item.description: The 2026 Met Gala, held at the Metropolitan Museum of Art, showcased Hollywood's elite embracing the theme 'Fashion is Art', coinciding with the museum's new Costume Art exhibition. The theme encourag...

item.title: Sassa Confirms Grant Payments Will Proceed Despite Postbank Service Changes
item.description: The South African Social Security Agency (Sassa) has reassured the public that all social grants will be paid on schedule despite recent service disruptions at Post Office branches caused by Postbank ...

item.title: Ghana Set to Host International Conference on Reparations Following UN Resolution
item.description: Ghana don announce say e go hold one big international conference for Accra from 17 to 19 June to discuss reparations for the transatlantic slave trade. Dis move come after the United Nations pass one...

item.title: Full List of 43 Public Universities Eligible for KUCCPS Placement and Funding
item.description: With the KUCCPS application deadline closing on May 6, 2026, the Kenyan government has released a complete list of 43 public universities eligible for funding through the Universities Fund. Students a...

item.title: Rivers police arrest 13 over journalist assault and other criminal activities
item.description: Rivers State Police have arrested at least 13 suspects in connection with the alleged assault of broadcast journalist Allwell Ene, who works with Megaletrics stations including Classic FM, Beat FM and...

item.title: Ebony Park Residents Organise Contributions to Restore Electricity After Year-Long Blackout
item.description: Residents of Ebony Park Phase 1 in Midrand have been without electricity for over a year after their local transformer was damaged due to overloading and illegal connections. In response, a WhatsApp g...

item.title: Kalita Maji, Former Domestic Worker, Secures Victory on BJP Ticket in West Bengal's Ausgram Constituency
item.description: In a remarkable story from West Bengal, Kalita Maji, who used to work as a domestic helper in four households earning a modest salary of around Rs 4,500 per month, has achieved a significant victory i...

item.title: Examining the Role of Pension Funds in Canada's Economic Future
item.description: John Rapley's column addresses the role of pension funds in Canada and their responsibility towards the country's long-term economic health. He argues that while Canada is wealthy, much of this wealth...

item.title: Supreme Court directs Comelec and DFA to comment on petition challenging automated vote counting for 2025 polls
item.description: The Supreme Court of the Philippines has ordered the Commission on Elections (Comelec) and the Department of Foreign Affairs (DFA) to file their comments on a petition questioning the automated counti...

item.title: Vernon trailer park residents remain without power amid rising temperatures
item.description: Residents of the Crown Villa mobile home park in Vernon, B.C., are entering their fourth week without electricity. The power was shut off on April 2 due to longstanding electrical hazards that Technic...

item.title: Tanzanian shilling weakens due to rising global fuel prices from Middle East tensions
item.description: The Tanzanian shilling has weakened against the US dollar after conflicts in the Middle East increased global fuel prices and raised demand for foreign currency among local importers. On April 28, 202...

item.title: Kenya Advises Its Citizens to Stay Safe Amid Rising Xenophobic Attacks in South Africa
item.description: The Kenyan government has issued a cautionary advisory to its citizens living and working in South Africa due to escalating xenophobic violence targeting foreigners. In a statement from the Kenya High...

item.title: CBSE Class 12 results show steady rise in high scorers and improved pass percentage over eight years
item.description: The report looks at the eight-year trend in CBSE Class 12 results and shows a clear rise in strong academic performance. In 2018, 12,737 students scored above 95 per cent, while 72,569 students crosse...
